AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who updated this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated By field indicates the user who updated this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Description
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A space to write additional related information.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A description is limited to 255 characters.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who updated this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated By field indicates the user who updated this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Tab
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: An indication that a tab is displayed within a window.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Tab indicates a tab that displays within a window.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record was last updated
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated field indicates the date that this record was updated.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Created By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who created this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created By field indicates the user who created this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Timeout (seconds)
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The timeout is the frequency (in seconds) which the ping for a cluster service is done.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The timeout is the frequency (in seconds) which the ping for a cluster service is done, i.e., it represents the amount of time that should pass for the cluster node in charge of a cluster service to reaffirm itself as the leader. It is also used as a reference for the rest of the nodes in the cluster to determine if the leader is no longer alive and therefore if a new node should be promoted as the leader of the cluster service. Please note that it will be required to restart all the nodes in the cluster if this value is changed in order to make the new value visible for the entire cluster.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Client
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Active
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: SMTP server account
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User Name
AD_COLUMN.HELP: Name that identifies the user in the System

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who updated this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated By field indicates the user who updated this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Created By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who created this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created By field indicates the user who created this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record was last updated
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated field indicates the date that this record was updated.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Creation Date
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record is completed.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Created By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who created this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created By field indicates the user who created this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Organization
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Organizational entity within client
AD_COLUMN.HELP: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Alert Status
AD_COLUMN.HELP: Status of the alert: New: The alert has been created and has not been reviewed yet. Read: The alert has been reviewed and user is waiting to be solved. Solved: The alert has been solved and is not an issue any more. Exception: The alert has been acknowledged but not action is going to be made to solve it.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Creation Date
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record is completed.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Table
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A dictionary table used for this tab that points to the database table.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Table indicates the table in which a field or fields reside.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Search Key
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A fast method for finding a particular record.
AD_COLUMN.HELP:
A search key allows for a fast method of finding a particular record. If you leave the search key empty, the system automatically creates a numeric number originating from a document sequence defined in the "Document Sequence" window. The sequence naming convention follows a rule "DocumentNo_TableName", where TableName is the actual name of the database table (e.g. C_Order).
Usually, company's internal identifiers for various records (products, customers, etc) are stored here.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Created By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who created this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created By field indicates the user who created this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Created By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who created this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created By field indicates the user who created this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Description
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A space to write additional related information.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A description is limited to 255 characters.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Client
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record was last updated
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated field indicates the date that this record was updated.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who updated this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated By field indicates the user who updated this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record was last updated
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated field indicates the date that this record was updated.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Name
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A non-unique identifier for a record/document often used as a search tool.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A more descriptive identifier (that does need to be unique) of a record/document that is used as a default search option along with the search key (that is unique and mostly shorter). It is up to 60 characters in length.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Client
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Security_Conn
AD_COLUMN.HELP: Connection security needed for the defined smtp server.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Password
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A secret code used to allow access to a specified window or tab.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Password indicates the Password for this User Id. Passwords are required to identify authorized users.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Client
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record was last updated
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated field indicates the date that this record was updated.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Creation Date
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record is completed.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Column
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A link to the database column of the table.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: Link to the database column of the table

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Active
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Subject
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The Subject of the mail
AD_COLUMN.HELP: References will be replaced by values: * @cus_ref@: The document reference of the customer * @our_ref@: The reference of the document * @cus_nam@: The name of the customer * @sal_nam@: The name of the sales rep. * @bp_nam@: The Business Partner name * @doc_date@: The document date * @doc_desc@: The document description * @doc_nextduedate@: The next due date (if document has associated any payment plan) * @doc_lastduedate@: The last due date (if document has associated any payment plan)

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Active
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Creation Date
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record is completed.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record was last updated
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated field indicates the date that this record was updated.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Default
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A value that is shown whenever a record is created.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Default Checkbox indicates if this record will be used as a default value.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Subject
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The Subject of the mail
AD_COLUMN.HELP: References will be replaced by values: * @cus_ref@: The document reference of the customer * @our_ref@: The reference of the document * @cus_nam@: The name of the customer * @sal_nam@: The name of the sales rep. * @bp_nam@: The Business Partner name * @doc_date@: The document date * @doc_desc@: The document description * @doc_nextduedate@: The next due date (if document has associated any payment plan) * @doc_lastduedate@: The last due date (if document has associated any payment plan)

AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Tab indicates a tab that displays within a window.

AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated By field indicates the user who updated this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Commercial Name
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Commercial Name of the Business Partner
AD_COLUMN.HELP: Commercial Name of the Business Partner

AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.

A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.
